messages.text: Well just keep in the back of your mind a number you might want for initial total board members and who you might have in mind to fill those seats.

It starts with 1, you. Add as needed /advised

BJ is a maybe
See if the startup helpers can pair you with someone useful

You know, there's this really savvy serial entrepreneur I heard about the other day, Jim Turner. Maybe we could see if he'd be interested in joining...

Too soon? I'm sorry, I really can't help myself.

Anywho, I get the sense that Baker wants to be at 3+ so there are balances for if he decides to go all evil mastermind. I agree with Eric's opinion on BJ if he's still in the game, and then ask the startup factory after that. I could see maybe adding one of the ISU related people (Kevin Moore, Steve Smith, Donna, etc.) but I could also see arguments for getting out of the ISU orbit.

:joy: :joy: :joy: :joy: . To be honest I actually wanted either Michael or Eric to serve as the position to represent the owners group, and then add one of the start up factory people (Peter hong/ Kevin Moore) and then an outside person as either BJ or Risha, whichever one likely start with BJ. In my opinion you start with 3 to get the initial CEO hire and then reserve the other 2 spots for outside entities/VC people
Do you not see yourself as the CEO?
I see myself as the temporary CEO to get stuff off the ground but not the permanent CEO. I don’t have the experience to take it the whole way there.
I believe I have much to learn and would need to be groomed (I hate that word) to be in that role

That's probably reasonable, though you hear about plenty of start ups that the people who started it just figured shit out and they were "the man" the whole time. I'm not saying we shouldn't consider bringing in a "professional ceo" I'm just saying don't necessarily rule yourself out of it either.

I think we should hear advice from our attorney as well as the startup factory folks and if it seems reasonable follow their recommendations. Also, I would be willing to serve on the board if I am asked to do so. Based on your above, I'm guessing what you want to do is have the CEO with majority of the power and the board with the power to redirect if necessary. Since you're going to start as the CEO, you're not wanting to be on the board. I'm guessing that's probably reasonable, though if/when you leave the CEO position, I think you should definitely represent the ownership group.

I suspect there are probably other ways that this could be structured as well, so I think you should get some feedback from the lawyers/startup factory. I think it's totally fine to have some checks and balances put in place, but I personally am willing to defer what, if anything, that looks like to you. I have thoughts, but I would also want to hear what the more experienced start-up folks would have to say

I don't think any of us are power hungry. I don't think anyone on the team is really demanding any leadership roles. Others can speak for themselves here, but while I would be willing to serve in a board/management type role, I am neither seeking it nor will I be upset if I don't receive such a position.

One thing that I will say is why we need an external CEO is we need to show we can work with external people. SL has really left a bad taste in people’s mouths. That’s what I got out of my start up factory meeting
And I think we are not familiar with VC and having somebody who knows that scene would be great.

And like I said I’m willing to hear anybody else’s opinion. These are just my thoughts with how we can politic our way into potential opportunities while having a CEO that would be ideal for the start up culture
